zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.於1504年出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福時，她的父親Sir Roger Dalton35歲，母親Anne Radcliffe25歲。她於1524年與Henry Hurst在英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1569年11月15日在英國貝德福爾亨洛福享年65歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾亨洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.于1504年出生在英国英格兰貝德福爾亨洛福时，她的父亲Sir Roger Dalton35岁，母亲Anne Radcliffe25岁。她于1524年与Henry Hurst在英国英格兰貝德福爾亨洛福结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1569年11月15日在英国貝德福爾亨洛福享年65岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾亨洛福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dalton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Dalton in Westmorland, Durham, Lancashire, Northumberland, and Yorkshire, from Old English dæl ‘valley’ (see Dale ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
History: The surname was taken to Ireland by English traders in the early 13th century .

variants.name: Daughton
meanings: English (Lincolnshire) and Irish (Kilkenny and Tipperary): variant of   Dalton  .

variants.name: Dale
meanings: English: from Middle English dal, dale, daile ‘dale, valley’ (Old English dæl, reinforced in northern England by the cognate Old Norse dalr), a topographic name for someone who lived in a valley, or a habitational name from any of numerous minor places called with this word, such as Dale in Cumbria and Yorkshire.
Norwegian: habitational name from a common farm named from Old Norse dali, the dative case of dalr ‘valley’.
Americanized form of German   Diehl  .

variants.name: Dolton
meanings: English: variant of   Dalton  .

variants.name: Daulton
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Dalton  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Henlow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Enslow  .

variants.name: Enslow
meanings: English: variant of   Anslow  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Regent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Polish, Czech, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic); French (Régent): status name or nickname from a vernacular form of Latin regens ‘ruling’, present participle of regere ‘to rule’. The usual meaning of the word is ‘one who governs in place of a sovereign (typically, one who is still a child or who is incapacitated through mental or other illness)’, but at certain medieval universities, regent was a status name for a member of the teaching faculty who held a particular position of authority. The surname could have denoted a servant of a regent in either sense, or it may have been a nickname for someone who behaved as if he was in charge. As a Jewish name it is artificial.

English (Suffolk): variant of Ridgeon (also found as Redgin and Redgen), a Suffolk form of Rigden . The addition of final -t may have been motivated by false association with the word regent (see 1 above).

variants.name: Rigden
meanings: English (Kent): from the Old |French and Middle English personal name Rikedun, a diminutive of Rikke (short form of Rickard ) + the Old French hypocoristic suffix -dun.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. was born in 1504, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Roger Dalton, was 35 and her mother, Anne Radcliffe, was 25. She married Henry Hurst in 1524, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 15 November 1569, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. est née en 1504, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Roger Dalton, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Anne Radcliffe, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Hurst en 1524, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 15 novembre 1569, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. 1504 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Roger Dalton 35 und ihre Mutter Anne Radcliffe 25. Sie hat 1524 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Henry Hurst geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 15. November 1569 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England und wurde in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. è nata il nel 1504 nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Roger Dalton, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Anne Radcliffe, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Henry Hurst nel 1524, nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 15 novembre 1569, nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a Henlow, Bedfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.が1504年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで生まれた時，父Sir Roger Daltonは35歳，母Anne Radcliffeは25歳であった。1524年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウでHenry Hurstと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1569年11月15日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで65歳で死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant.(이)가 1504년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Roger Dalton(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Radcliffe(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1524년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 Henry Hurst(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1569년 11월 15일 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. nasceu em 1504, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Roger Dalton, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Anne Radcliffe, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Hurst em 1524, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 15 de novembro de 1569, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. родилась в 1504 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Roger Dalton было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Radcliffe, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Hurst в 1524 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 15 ноября 1569 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Regent Agnes de Dalton of Henlow Charlemagne descendant. nació en 1504, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Roger Dalton, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Anne Radcliffe, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Henry Hurst en 1524, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 15 de noviembre de 1569, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.
